summary: أعلنت مجموعة "GROW" للاستثمار، المتخصصة في إدارة الأصول على المستوى العالمي ومقرها الرئيسي في شنغهاي، بإجمالي أصول خاضعة للإدارة يبلغ نحو 1.5 مليار دولار، عن حصولها على الموافقة المبدئية من سلطة تنظيم الخدمات المالية في أبوظبي العالمي، وذلك على طلب تصريح الخدمات المالية.

title: Humanoid robots may be about to break the 100-metre sprint record
summary: Robots can now run a half-marathon faster than humans and are rapidly homing in on the men's 100-metre sprint record. But why are companies so keen to create speedy robots that have no obvious application in homes or factories?

title: We may finally have a cure for many different autoimmune conditions
summary: A revolutionary cancer treatment is now being applied to a wide range of autoimmune disorders. Columnist Michael Le Page finds it is proving to be even more effective than expected

title: Why the keto diet could be a revolutionary way to treat mental illness
summary: You may think of the high-fat, low-carb eating plan as a faddish way to lose weight. But the keto diet is now being used to tackle conditions from severe depression to bipolar disorder and anorexia, with transformative results

title: Giant Arctic continent launched dinosaurs to world domination
summary: Coincident with the rise of the dinosaurs, a large landmass filled most of the Arctic circle, potentially contributing to global cooling that advantaged the famous reptiles

title: 10,000 new planets found hidden in NASA telescope data
summary: NASA's Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite has been searching for exoplanets since its launch in 2018, and it turns out it may have found plenty more of them than we had thought

title: 100-year-old assumption about the universe may soon be overturned
summary: Physicists have long assumed that the universe is uniform at very large scales, but evidence is emerging this is wrong and suggests a way to resolve some of the biggest cosmological mysteries

title: Gravity's strength measured more reliably than ever before
summary: Measuring the strength of gravity is extraordinarily difficult, and different experiments have always disagreed – but a new test is paving the way to finally understanding nature’s most enigmatic force

title: Symptoms of early dementia reversed by bespoke treatment plans
summary: People with cognitive decline or early-stage dementia saw their symptoms improve when given bespoke treatment plans that targeted their personal nutritional deficiencies, ongoing infections and environmental exposures

title: QBox theory may offer glimpse of reality deeper than quantum realm
summary: Physicists have long suspected that there is a layer of physical reality beneath quantum theory and a new mathematical model unveils just how strange it might be

title: Is stem cell therapy about to transform medicine and reverse ageing?
summary: A clinical trial to reverse age-related vision conditions using stem cell treatment could finally deliver on the promise of a major discovery in ageing and regeneration made 20 years ago, says columnist Graham Lawton

title: Catching a cold can delay cancer from spreading to the lungs
summary: Infecting mice with RSV, a common virus that causes cold-like symptoms, prevented breast cancer cells from reaching their lungs. This was due to the release of proteins that stop viruses from replicating in the lungs also making it harder for cancer cells to seed new tumours

title: Huge study reveals how Epstein-Barr virus may cause multiple sclerosis
summary: The Epstein-Barr virus seems to affect gene expression and cell signalling in a way that causes the autoimmune condition multiple sclerosis

title: Striking photo essay examines deadly spread of dengue fever in Nepal
summary: Photographer Yuri Segalerba explores how dengue has spread to Nepal's Himalayan districts, and how locals are fighting back

summary: The idea that EV batteries age poorly is a misconception – and a new report has found they often outlive the cars themselves

title: Can you slow ageing with your diet? A new book gives it a go
summary: Discovering he is getting old before his time, David Cox tries to lower his biological age by changing his diet in a helpful new book, The Age Code, says Graham Lawton

summary: Some seemingly simple sequences of multiplication and addition grow so quickly that they question the very foundations of mathematics. In doing so, they demand a whole new level of logic

title: Parrot uses his broken beak to become a dominant male
summary: An injured kea with just half a beak has used what's left as a weapon that gives him dominance over a captive colony of the birds

title: Can you determine your personalised stress score?
summary: “I’m stressed” is a phrase that many of us use, but now there are ways to shed light on how stressed you actually are

title: Brushing your teeth in hospital could prevent catching a bad infection
summary: Most hospital patients don't brush their teeth regularly, but doing so could cut their risk of developing pneumonia during their stay

title: Electric vehicle owners could earn thousands by supporting power grid
summary: Electric vehicles could store renewable energy when there is excess supply and give it back to the grid when demand peaks, but car companies disagree on the best way to do that

title: The rise, the fall and the rebound of cyclic cosmology
summary: Cyclic cosmology, or the big bounce, is the idea that the universe will eventually crunch back together and then go through another big bang. Columnist Leah Crane finds that, appropriately, it’s coming back

summary: The Lyrid meteor shower will soon hit its peak. Here's how to spot it, including by using the New Scientist stargazing companion

summary: An imaginative and compelling book reveals how radio waves help us tune in to our universe – and even search for alien civilisations, says Karmela Padavic-Callaghan

summary: Forty years after the world’s biggest nuclear disaster, the safety of Chernobyl hangs in the balance – though not because of the radiation risk

title: نيوزيويك: حصار أمريكي في بيئة معقدة .. لماذا يبدو تطويق إيران بحرياً مهمة شبه مستحيلة؟
summary: صعوبة فرض حصار بحري على إيران لا تنبع من توازن القوى التقليدي، بل من اختلاف قواعد اللعبة نفسها. فبينما تعتمد القوى الكبرى على التفوق العسكري المباشر، تراهن طهران على تكتيكات مرنة، مدعومة بجغرافيا معقدة وخبرة طويلة في الحروب غير المتكافئة. بعد فشل مفاوضات باكستان التي لم تدم الا 21 ساعة فقط، عاد الت…

title: تعاون جديد بين أوليمبيك ليون الفرنسي ودينامو مصر
summary: وقع نادي دينامو مصر شراكة حصرية مع نادي أوليمبيك ليون الفرنسي، حتى عام 2028. وبناء على اتفاقية الشراكة سوف يتم إرسال أبرز اللاعبين الموجودين في قطاع ناشئين نادي دينامو مصر لنادي ليون، خاصة في عمر ما تحت 18 عاما، وذلك لاكتساب الخبرات. كما سيتم تطوير قطاع الناشئين و المدربين بنادي دينامو مصر، كما أن …
body: وقع نادي دينامو مصر شراكة حصرية مع نادي أوليمبيك ليون الفرنسي، حتى عام 2028. وبناء على اتفاقية الشراكة سوف يتم إرسال أبرز اللاعبين الموجودين في قطاع ناشئين نادي دينامو مصر لنادي ليون، خاصة في عمر ما تحت 18 عاما، وذلك لاكتساب الخبرات. كما سيتم تطوير قطاع الناشئين و المدربين بنادي دينامو مصر، كما أن هناك اتفاق آخر علي نطاق الشرق الأوسط و بعض دول أفريقيا لإطلاق مشاريع خلال ٢٠٤٢/٢٠٢٥ تهدف الي تطوير الناشئين بالتعاون مع الانديه و الأكاديميات الموجودة سواء في الشرق الأوسط و أفريقيا لاكتشاف أبرز المواهب. ومن جانبه أكد علي عبد المنعم في تصريحات لقناة نادي ليون الفرنسي قائلا “سعداء بتلك الخطوه حيث انها تتماشي مع سياسه شركه دينامو لكره القدم نحو تطوير عناصر اللعبة بداية من اللاعبين الذين سيتم تدريبهم وفقا للسياسه التدريبيه لنادي ليون فضلا عن منحهم فترات معايشه بشكل مستمر من خلال النادي في فرنسا و نتمني ونثق أن يكون لدينا لاعبين من اولادنا قريبا جدا في فرنسا. واختتم تصريحاته قائلا، تم الاتفاق علي العمل على انتشار الشراكات الاستراتيجية من هذا النحو في منطقه الشرق الأوسط و بعض دول أفريقيا و بالفعل لدينا زياره خلال شهر اكتوبر في أفريقيا لإتمام أولى خطوات التعاون في هذا الصدد”. The post تعاون جديد بين أوليمبيك ليون الفرنسي ودينامو مصر appeared first on ملاعب المصري اليوم .
